content.text: Google Cloud Vertex AI is Google's managed AI platform that hosts Claude through a partnership with Anthropic. Rather than calling Anthropic's API directly, you call Vertex AI endpoints using your Google Cloud credentials (service account or Application Default Credentials). Your requests and responses stay within Google Cloud's network and compliance boundary.

Vertex AI is the right choice when: your organization runs on GCP, you need GDPR data residency enforced by Google's DPA, you want to colocate Claude with other GCP services (BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow), or your compliance team requires GCP-native audit trails and VPC Service Controls.
. Always pin the version — the same model name may map to different weights at different dates, and floating aliases are not guaranteed stable.

attrs.alt: Google Cloud Vertex AI integration architecture showing Your App, Vertex AI Model Garden, Claude, IAM, and key features

content.text: Vertex AI uses Google Cloud's standard authentication: a service account key or Workload Identity (the preferred approach on GKE). The Anthropic SDK's
library, which reads Application Default Credentials (ADC) from the environment.

# Create a service account
gcloud iam service-accounts create claude-vertex-sa   --display-name="Claude Vertex AI service account"

# Grant the minimum Vertex AI role
gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding MY_PROJECT   --member="serviceAccount:claude-vertex-sa@MY_PROJECT.iam.gserviceaccount.com"   --role="roles/aiplatform.user"

# Download a key for local dev (NOT for production — use Workload Identity instead)
gcloud iam service-accounts keys create vertex-key.json   --iam-account=claude-vertex-sa@MY_PROJECT.iam.gserviceaccount.com

export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS="$(pwd)/vertex-key.json"
On GKE, use Workload Identity to bind a Kubernetes service account to the GCP service account — no key file required:

# Bind KSA to GSA
gcloud iam service-accounts add-iam-policy-binding   claude-vertex-sa@MY_PROJECT.iam.gserviceaccount.com   --role="roles/iam.workloadIdentityUser"   --member="serviceAccount:MY_PROJECT.svc.id.goog[claude-ns/claude-ksa]"

kubectl annotate serviceaccount claude-ksa   --namespace claude-ns   iam.gke.io/gcp-service-account=claude-vertex-sa@MY_PROJECT.iam.gserviceaccount.com

import anthropic

client = anthropic.AnthropicVertex(
    region="us-central1",            # Vertex AI region
    project_id="my-gcp-project",     # Your GCP project ID
)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-opus-4-8@20251101",    # Vertex model ID with version
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain VPC Service Controls."}],
)
print(message.content[0].text)
can also be set via environment variables
, useful for twelve-factor apps where config comes from the environment.
Vertex AI uses a different model ID format than the Direct API or Bedrock. The version timestamp is required — you must specify which model release to use.

) pins you to a specific model snapshot. When Anthropic releases a new version, your application continues using the pinned version until you update the model ID. This prevents unexpected behavior changes from silent model upgrades.

import anthropic

client = anthropic.AnthropicVertex(
    region="us-central1",
    project_id="my-gcp-project",
)

with client.messages.stream(
    model="claude-opus-4-8@20251101",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a short poem about cloud computing."}],
) as stream:
    for text in stream.text_stream:
        print(text, end="", flush=True)
print()
Streaming is identical to the Direct API — the
client translates the Vertex AI streaming response format into the same SSE event stream the Anthropic SDK exposes.
Using the Vertex AI SDK Directly
If you prefer the native Vertex AI SDK (without the Anthropic wrapper), you call the
endpoint with the Anthropic request body. This is lower-level but gives direct access to Vertex AI SDK features like request tracing.

from google.cloud import aiplatform
from google.protobuf import json_format
from google.protobuf.struct_pb2 import Value
import json

aiplatform.init(project="my-gcp-project", location="us-central1")

endpoint = aiplatform.Endpoint(
    endpoint_name="publishers/anthropic/models/claude-opus-4-8@20251101"
)

instance = {
    "anthropic_version": "vertex-2023-10-16",
    "max_tokens": 1024,
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello from Vertex SDK"}],
}

response = endpoint.predict(instances=[instance])
result = response.predictions[0]
print(result["content"][0]["text"])

VPC Service Controls (VPC-SC) create a security perimeter around GCP services, preventing data from leaving the perimeter even if a service account is compromised. For Claude on Vertex AI, VPC-SC means that
calls can only come from within approved VPC networks or access levels.
in Google Cloud Console → Security → VPC Service Controls
to allow calls only from your corporate network CIDRs or approved identities
With VPC-SC active, any attempt to call Vertex AI from outside the perimeter (e.g. a developer's home network without VPN, or a compromised credential) results in an
error. This is the strongest data exfiltration defense available on GCP.
Vertex AI writes metrics to Cloud Monitoring and optionally logs full request/response pairs to Cloud Logging. Enable logging in the Vertex AI console under your model endpoint settings.

from google.cloud import monitoring_v3
import time

client = monitoring_v3.MetricServiceClient()
project_name = f"projects/my-gcp-project"

interval = monitoring_v3.TimeInterval()
now = time.time()
interval.end_time.seconds = int(now)
interval.start_time.seconds = int(now) - 3600   # last hour

results = client.list_time_series(
    request={
        "name": project_name,
        "filter": (
            'metric.type="aiplatform.googleapis.com/prediction/online/latencies"'
        ),
        "interval": interval,
        "view": monitoring_v3.ListTimeSeriesRequest.TimeSeriesView.FULL,
    }
)

for result in results:
    print(result)
Vertex AI's primary advantage over the Direct API is colocation with other GCP services. Common patterns:
: Deploy a Cloud Run service that calls
— no VMs, scales to zero, uses Workload Identity automatically
: Call Claude from a BigQuery remote function to enrich structured data with LLM-generated annotations (e.g. sentiment, classification)
: Read documents from GCS, batch-process with Claude, write results back to GCS — all within the same VPC perimeter
: Async pipeline for high-volume document processing — Pub/Sub buffers spikes, Cloud Run calls Vertex AI at a controlled rate

# Example: Cloud Run service calling AnthropicVertex
# main.py — deploy with: gcloud run deploy claude-service --source .

import anthropic
import os
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify

app = Flask(__name__)
client = anthropic.AnthropicVertex(
    region=os.environ["VERTEX_REGION"],       # from Cloud Run env var
    project_id=os.environ["GCP_PROJECT"],
)

@app.route("/summarize", methods=["POST"])
def summarize():
    text = request.json.get("text", "")
    message = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-opus-4-8@20251101",
        max_tokens=512,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Summarize: {text}"}],
    )
    return jsonify({"summary": message.content[0].text})
Comparing Vertex AI to Bedrock and Direct API
Prompt Caching is supported on Vertex AI for eligible Claude models. The interface is identical to the Direct API — add
to content blocks you want cached. The
client handles the Vertex-specific headers automatically.

import anthropic

client = anthropic.AnthropicVertex(
    region="us-central1",
    project_id="my-gcp-project",
)

KNOWLEDGE_BASE = open("company_docs.txt").read()   # large static document

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-opus-4-8@20251101",
    max_tokens=1024,
    system=[
        {
            "type": "text",
            "text": KNOWLEDGE_BASE,
            "cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"},
        },
        {
            "type": "text",
            "text": "You are a helpful assistant. Answer only from the documents above.",
        },
    ],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the PTO policy?"}],
)

usage = message.usage
print(f"Cache write: {getattr(usage, 'cache_creation_input_tokens', 0)}")
print(f"Cache read:  {getattr(usage, 'cache_read_input_tokens', 0)}")
print(message.content[0].text)

Each Vertex AI region maintains a separate cache. If you serve users from multiple regions, prime each region's cache during initialization rather than relying on the first user request to warm it. Cache TTL is 5 minutes by default on Vertex AI.

Vertex AI Batch Prediction processes large volumes of requests asynchronously, writing results to Cloud Storage. This is equivalent to Bedrock Batch and the Direct API's Batch API, but uses GCP-native tooling.

from google.cloud import aiplatform

aiplatform.init(project="my-gcp-project", location="us-central1")

# Input JSONL must be in GCS
job = aiplatform.BatchPredictionJob.create(
    job_display_name="claude-sentiment-batch",
    model_name="publishers/anthropic/models/claude-opus-4-8@20251101",
    instances_format="jsonl",
    gcs_source_uris=["gs://my-bucket/input/requests.jsonl"],
    gcs_destination_prefix="gs://my-bucket/output/",
    machine_type="n1-standard-4",
)

job.wait()

if job.state == aiplatform.gapic.JobState.JOB_STATE_SUCCEEDED:
    print(f"Output at: {job.output_info.gcs_output_directory}")
else:
    print(f"Job failed: {job.error}")
Each line in the input JSONL must conform to the Vertex AI prediction format:
. Batch jobs run on Vertex AI infrastructure and are subject to project-level quotas, not on-demand quotas.
Error Handling and Retries on Vertex AI
client surfaces errors as the same
types as the Direct API, so existing retry logic transfers. When using the Vertex AI SDK directly, errors come as
Fix the request; do not retry

Vertex AI quotas for Claude are configured per project per region. They are separate from your Direct API or Bedrock quotas. Default limits are lower than Bedrock for new projects; submit a quota increase request in the GCP console before load testing.

Unlike Bedrock, Vertex AI does not have a built-in cross-region routing feature. For higher throughput, deploy your application to multiple GCP regions and load-balance at the application layer, sending EU traffic to
Vertex AI inherits GCP's compliance certifications. The key ones relevant to Claude deployments:
: Information security and privacy management — automatically covered by using a GCP-compliant service
: Security, availability, confidentiality controls — GCP audit reports available via the Compliance Reports Manager
: Sign a BAA with Google for your project; Vertex AI appears on Google's HIPAA-covered services list
: GCP's DPA covers Vertex AI as a sub-processor; request data is processed in the region you specify, satisfying data residency requirements
: GCP is PCI Level 1 certified; Vertex AI services can be included in scope with proper network segmentation
to capture Admin Activity and Data Access logs. These logs record who called which model, when, and from where — essential for compliance audits and incident investigation.
Google Cloud Vertex AI hosts Claude within GCP's compliance and security boundary, making it the right choice for GCP-centric organizations. The
SDK client mirrors the standard API interface — only the model ID format (

) and client constructor differ from Direct API code. Key advantages: VPC Service Controls for data exfiltration prevention, Workload Identity for credential-free authentication on GKE, versioned model IDs for stable inference, and native integration with Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging, BigQuery, and Cloud Storage. For multi-cloud deployments, pair Vertex AI (EU region) with Bedrock (US region) for geographic redundancy with separate compliance boundaries.
